page: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v4 is an EASv4-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with x86_64 architecture, providing 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per core, and includes 885 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and strong image processing capabilities in Geekbench. Passmark memory testing also ranks in the top 25%, reflecting the high-density memory subsystem. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average tier. For language model workloads, the server demonstrates strong text generation speeds with smaller 135M models, though performance drops to the bottom 25% for prompt processing on larger 70B models.

Given its high memory allocation of 512 GB relative to 32 vCPUs, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. The combination of dedicated AMD EPYC cores, a strong memory subsystem, and 885 GB of local storage makes it well-suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise applications, and memory-heavy data processing tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v4 is an EASv4-family virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a 3.525 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture and running under Microsoft Hyper-V, this server features 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core, alongside 885 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance, strong image processing, and strong Passmark memory scores, while multi-core compute and memory bandwidth remain average. This memory-optimized profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense workloads. It is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, including large databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v4 is an EASv4 virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 885 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v4 is an EASv4 virtual machine with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 885 GB local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-density RAM for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores
512 GB of RAM providing a high 16 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio
885 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU, image processing, and memory benchmark performance
Optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
